title: Add FreeBSD support for buildah run
body:
<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read and understood our contributing guidelines
(https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) as well as ensuring
that all your commits are signed with `git commit -s`.
-->

#### What type of PR is this?

<!--
Please label this pull request according to what type of issue you are
addressing, especially if this is a release targeted pull request.

Uncomment only one `/kind <>` line, hit enter to put that in a new line, and
remove leading whitespace from that line:
-->

> /kind other

#### What this PR does / why we need it:

This adds support for running containers on FreeBSD, including support for volume mounts and CNI-based networking. This stack of diffs is missing a required change to github.com/opencontainers/runtime-tools which enables OCI spec generation for FreeBSD which is awaiting review (https://github.com/opencontainers/runtime-tools/pull/746). 

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
None
```

[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED]

comments.body: The smoke test is failing since there are no new tests. The linux part of the diff should not introduce any functional changes and I'm still figuring out what the best approach for testing freebsd buildah. I can add ```[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED]``` but not sure if I need to add that to all the commits or just the last one?
Adding [NO NEW TESTS NEEDED] to the github.com should be enough, I believe.

> It looks like there is still a lot of duplication between run_freebsd.go and run_linux.go.
> Can we consolidate this more to only have the actual difference in these files.

There is a lot of similar code around volume mounts - I'll work through that and consolidate. This is likely to be in small pieces to get a signal from CI that I haven't broken anything.

The failing check looks like a transient:
```
[+1759s] # $ /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/buildah/tests/./../bin/buildah rm time="2022-06-19T10:03:58Z" level=warning msg="Failed, retrying in 2s ... (1/3). Error: copying system image from manifest list: parsing image configuration: Get \"https://cdn02.quay.io/sha256/b0/b0757c55a1fdbb59c378fd34dde3e12bd25f68094dd69546cf5ca00ddbaa7a33?Expires=1655633638&Signature=XxdWm6xJTnN0KMze1BhdfeokentQb3-LMl0D9HhJmWi8femnlufaHFuLpVriBqBmb7Vo9WzXUcJG5hwiU6tNtyOyDN4hX9~ktC29LzDeslabDwyC3gSw7zN~yIGXoB0N~dbKl~P~J1m2W6equTYveh8mET-z5QPvgqtqNL4L1M68EU2bg2mFky02CRy1ndc~vbAXw6FwwXfQ3fIHqZ8GS3JmYqgesxN6kl6tcdEXbAZ6clKzJD-VtMFMEE2ysyFQBz5V8EW3KTV7BnnwhBCsed1eqG1vLhiAVNQiIxRMPQfK6eDWVnOa2p-bQRigJQCIFHw7ZL6IBoedY2Y~muziOg__&Key-Pair-Id=APKAJ67PQLWGCSP66DGA\": read tcp 10.128.0.13:45120->108.156.91.78:443: read: connection reset by peer" docker-working-container
```
Individual commits makes this much easier to review, so I am in favour of that.

I hope that the invidividual commits help with review. I also thought they might help to simplify merge conflicts since most are just moves of unmodified (or nearly unmodified) code. Certainly open to squashing it down when people are happy with the review if that will make the history cleaner.
@flouthoc @Luap99 PTAL
Rebased against today's main

Not at all a complete review yet but just a small doubt @dfr so all the commits with title `Move x to run_common.go` are intact movement of function to a common file or can they include changes in between as well ?  Just asking to make sure cause i saw changes specific to `freebsd` in 6061d5828a3c5bc04f57f3d746c4ecf676a52760 so just wanna make sure that we are not leaking any diff in `run_common.go` and they are just intact movement of functions.

A few of the diffs which move code to run_common.go do include small changes to support the port, e.g. using nullfs instead of bind for volume mounts (by adding declarations to the define package). Also, I remember working around the fact that Spec.Linux is nil for FreeBSD containers. I could separate that out if it would be helpful?

I will take a pass through your comments tomorrow, thanks for taking a look.
I split up a couple of the mount-related commits to separate copying from functional change and also addressed some of the other feedback.
rebased
@nalind @flouthoc @Luap99 PTAL
I noticed an unwanted addition to a commit log message deep in this stack and re-pushed - there should be no functional change.
Which reminds me, do people still want to squash this down? I think everything after 'Add run support for FreeBSD' could be reduced down to two commits as suggested earlier in the thread.

> Which reminds me, do people still want to squash this down? I think everything after 'Add run support for FreeBSD' could be reduced down to two commits as suggested earlier in the thread.

I am fine with multiple commits.  It makes things easier when it is time to bisect
@nalind @flouthoc @Luap99 @giuseppe @vrothberg PTAL
Rebased.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *dfr*, *flouthoc*, *giuseppe*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fbuildah). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [flouthoc,giuseppe,rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
/retest

@dfr: Cannot trigger testing until a trusted user reviews the PR and leaves an `/ok-to-test` message.

<details>

In response to [this](https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/4059#issuecomment-1172369470):

>/retest


Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>
/ok-to-test
/retest
/retest
@dfr Needs a rebase.  Please make sure you use fmt.Errorf and not "errors.New, errors.Wrap"
Rebased. There were quite a few conflicts from the errors changes so I would appreciate a second pair of eyes to make sure I didn't miss anything.
Not sure whats going on with the Test Vendoring CI - this PR doesn't modify go.mod

> Not sure whats going on with the Test Vendoring CI - this PR doesn't modify go.mod

Do not import `github.com/pkg/errors` just use `errors`, see https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/4091

> > Not sure whats going on with the Test Vendoring CI - this PR doesn't modify go.mod
> 
> Do not import `github.com/pkg/errors` just use `errors`, see #4091

Ah - I see a stray import in run_freebsd.go. Thanks for the pointer!
Looks like a rebase might be needed @dfr
Rebased

LGTM
@flouthoc PTAL
/lgtm
